prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "平板支撑交替抬腿" (plank with alternating leg raise) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person holding a forearm plank with one leg lifted: forearms on the ground (elbows under shoulders), body in a straight line from heels to head. The LEFT LEG is on the ground with the left toes supporting the body. The RIGHT LEG is lifted straight up off the ground, knee fully extended, with the right foot hovering about 15-20 cm above the floor. Hips remain level (no rotation). Glutes engaged. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: rectus abdominis and obliques (anti-extension and anti-rotation core work) and gluteus maximus of the lifted leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: shoulder stabilizers and quadriceps. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腹直肌 / 腹斜肌" pointing to the rectus abdominis and obliques; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus of the lifted leg; "肩稳定肌 / 股四头肌" pointing to the shoulder stabilizers and quadriceps. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
